item.title: Potential double top on 73.8K — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Potential double top on 73.8K

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-13 18:28 UTC

OP

Last thread: >>61938409

Its been almost 2 weeks and we didn't breach top of last bullrun's "first selloff". IF we will fall from here, and won't touch 90K ish anymore, then now would be a very perfect time to short.

Early replies (first 3)

61979960
>>61979788
Again, a note for newfags. See those 2 tops touching the 78.8K line? Thats a potential double top.
>double top is extremely bearish

And if we fall from here, confirming those double top, then the only thing that follow is a black swan event.

61979972
>>61979960
>78.8K lines
typo, sorry. What i meant was 73.8K red line

61979998
>>61979788
There are still a few 4hr candles that can make this a fakeout to pile on more shorts. Anything is possible in cryptoland. Double tops aren't 100% confirmation every time, and until we break the support line of the trough, it's not a confirmed double.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61980004
The most obvious bulltrap I've seen in my life. And happening on friday, it was beautiful. Quintaessential crypto manipulation + all the macro.

You know that all the bad news come on friday once American markets close, right? The pump has already lost its momentum, just ready for the crash. And TACO monday won't do shit this time.

55K, HERE WE GO!

61980049
>>61980004
wow, you're going to be rich with all of the shorts you have in place, anon! You do have shorts in place at such an obvious bulltrap, right, anon?

61980060
>>61979998
I agree as well. Anything is possible, thats why if you guys DO put a short position right now, do it with a stoploss limit

>support line
Is an ascending line, crossing 66K. I think its a good gamble to short now and dump some more short bags when we eventually cross it

>>61980004
Ah yea, right... the americans. Then we just have to see what happens this Friday EST

61980163
>>61980060
Just look at what happens every friday. If you open a short on friday and close on sunday night, you would have made a lot of money almost every week.

Now, add the double top and the Iranian war mess. They've been in full panic mode all week.

Just have a good stop loss and that's it. It's totally worth it.

>>61980049
The hype long has been open for weeks. Printing money. I added all those shorts just at the double top. Let's see what happens.

61980188
>>61980004
Just be aware that you are currently trading the majority opinion on biz and the entire normie sphere.

61980237
>>61980188
Bro, based digits, but who cares? It's all about probabilities. Just put a stop loss and go outside and have some beers with your friends. I'm in Europe, it's 18:30. At 19:30 I'm gonna meet my friends and have drinks and a cheese table among other snacks.

My stop loss are ready. I have 0 worries. Look at one of my other shorts, I didn't show them all. Shorted SUI at 1,03. Already printing. And I don't care.

61980270
>>61979788
>harga terakhir
>translate from indonesian
pic related

61980284
>>61980237

When I see a massive amount of people that generally don't make any money trading say one thing I generally do the opposite. It works 90% of the time. The masses don't buy as low as they want, and they don't sell as high as they want.

>The masses never win

61980309

>Ape stack line

KEKAROOO

I see it more often than not and generally from not very intelligent people. This happened last cycle too. I've already seen it over and over. You should have seen how many people missed BTC at 16k because it was "for sure" going under 10k. Everyone who counter traded that won.

61980346
Look at this beautiful chart. OIL. You see that bounce. Just at the same time as the double top. It's FRIDAY. Here comes the American hubrys. TACO. Everything has come together.

By the way, make sure you take some gains before monday. You know TACO man.

61980589

item.title: Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri… — 20 replies — 29.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-13 17:56 UTC

OP

Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tried this though. I haven't heard of anyone else doing these.

Early replies (first 3)

61975699
Dude if you ask AI to pick some crypto for you it will answer with:
>BTC
>ETH
>Cardano (lol)
>XRP
>Link
all bottom of the barrel shit crypto that will make you no money in this life unless you are already rich in which case I guess putting a huge bag on BTC wouldn't be wrong

AI has no insight it aggregates the average data, so you just got the average business idea from midwits on reddit for the current timeline, no one pays for that stuff and no one serious builds that stuff
You are literally much better off using your monkey brain and think of something

61975726
>>61975699
Yeah, AI is pretty bad for predicting anything. I was just curious how far off it was with these suggestions and if anyone would offer better ideas. I was hoping it would give an edge, anything to even the playing field given the shit economy and housing market.

61975829
>>61975625
#1 sounds good. 2 and 3, I wouldn’t bother.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61975863
>>61975625
1 sounds good if you are a member of reddit, if I open a small business today I literally get swarmed with invoicing/crm/bullshit sw made from actual engineers from actual companies that have actual tech support for as low as 5/10$ per month or something retarded like that.
No I don't want to pay the same money to some anon scumbag that "solves" problems by feeding them to a shitty AI

61975894
>>61975863
Can you recommend anything better? I'm willing to work on anything, just need something good to focus on beyond my current engineer job, which is kind of a dead end gig given that most people are in their 40s-50s at the company and I'm still just 29.

61975909
>>61975863
> pay a boomer company an overpriced monthly fee for your niche SaaS software
> or hire an ai bro to build a perfect clone of it for a one time fee
Gee I wonder which is the better idea here

61975987
>>61975894
told you, use your brain
find literally anything that pays money download real world data and run the numbers. Run the numbers until your preconceptions melt away and you are able to see the real constraints. After a bit you should find an angle that is fresh and underplayed (not hard since most business run on autopilot) that's your play.
>BUUUUUUUT what specifically?
fuck if I know, how about transporting refrigerated medicine + supermarket supplies?
There is people that make a living doing that, why can't you
>BOOOOORING
ye everything is boring at the start. You start liking it when you have more success than others and money comes in

61975993
>deepseek

unc moment

61976126
>>61975987
Thanks, yeah I guess the tricky thing is finding what can scale well and isn't impossible to do while working a full time job already. Consulting or something online I can do when I'm still on the clock.

>>61975993
what's currently better? I thought it was still as good as chatgpt's paid version if you toggle Deepthink

61976444
>>61975625
there are more than a few companies selling these ai services, why would a business take the product of joe nobody instead of theirs

61976535
>>61976126
That was one year ago. The top ai spot has changed like five times since then

61976580
I literally do 1 and 2 as part of my job. I have several years' experience with ML, LLMs, RAG, as well as fine tuning models to specific tasks.

It's near impossible to create a business out of it. You know all those retards who make videos on YouTube making yet another RAG tutorial? Or muh content creators on tiktok which display snippets of them following a tutorial and larping they are a consultant? Or those retards spamming utterly retarded AI slop on LinkedIn? The only reason they are wasting time on that is because they are desperately trying to get some retard to cold call them for their business. I bet most of them never had a client.

It's nightmarishly difficult to find customers, either you have small-medium companies which don't give a fuck, and they aren't going to pay you much, or you have large companies which already have internal departments committed to that and the best you can do is compete with 500 million jeets to wageslave there.

61976840
>>61975625
>Hey AI what business should I start?
>What about make work using AI?

There's downward pressure on wages and a surplus of retard labor meaning you can get a wage to do any of this shit for cheap. They won't spend money on "workflow consultation" while the economy is in free fall. Not once will AI tell you learn how to fix and replace water treatment systems because you cannot shoehorn AI into the service.

>>61975894
If anyone had a good business idea how would it benefit them to share? If you don't even have an idea to start from, implying you have experience with nothing, what good would an idea be to you if you even had one?

61976864
>plumbing
>fix some shit
>show butt crack
>drink all day
>make money
>fuck house wifes and singles all day everyday

61976993
>>61976840
It's not like we're making the next Google over there. This is just extra income to make on top of a job. I'm just wondering where's good to focus if AI can't help come up with anything realistic.

61977338
>>61976840
This is why /biz/ sucks now. I remember ideas would be openly discussed all the time before

61979388
>>61976993
>>61977338
75% of wealth is held by boomers and >50 gen x. You need to focus on extracting wealth from boomers and you don't need AI for that. These demographics normally spend money on healthcare and their homes. Learning some sort of trade and doing better work or more skilled work than immigrants is the easiest path. Buy a bucket truck, lol you cannot afford that, or a used lift and trim trees, dig wells, service septic. Fuck it I'm about to give you real fucking gold. Amerigas fucking sucks because they charge service fees for storage of tanks you don't actually own. If you could get a truck, cdl driver with hazmat, you could drive around filling large tanks, servicing and selling tanks that customers own and undercut americuntgas. I live and know the alternative to this and it sucks, there's a market there and you can get your own storage facility to make brick and mortar sales on top of scheduled delivery. You need business ideas that work for a failing empire, which includes decentralization of services.

You want to make big bucks short term? Buy 10k rounds of m193 & m855 or ss109. Sit on it for 45-90 days and you can resell for 50%+. Buy federal hst 9mm and 40 police marked boxes in bulk and you can make similar or greater profits at the risk of fewer customers for "premium" ammunition. All this requires capital and you physically doing something. But ammo scalping post scarcity just requires some storage space, up front cost and is infinitely scalable up and down.

61979438
>>61979388
I hesitate for these lower effort threads as it often comes down to how do I print money rather than work? I've discussed elements of business here before and gotten the feeling most of biz has little meaningful business experience defaulting to algorithmic hive mind in discussion. Biz anons regularly cannot find a job and is on the brink of starvation. My goal is to buy a storage facility near a boomer stronghold and make an electronic entry parking lot for boats and similar machines to charge storage fees.

61979778
>>61979388
>>61979438
Thanks for this. I guess I wouldn't be able to do this since I'm working a full time job. I'd have to find something that's possible from home, like making digital products/content creation/consulting.
Currently making pretty good money from a tech job and investments. I just wanted to diversify and increase my income to invest more without taking too much risk.
I guess that's why most of /biz/ just sticks to their job and clicking button investments instead of manual labor businesses or ammo scalping.

61980357
>>61979438
Great idea. Boat/RV storage is a cash cow for boomers.

item.title: Is BTC just a store of value or more? — 64 replies — 91.0/hr

Is BTC just a store of value or more?

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-03-13 17:55 UTC

OP

People say BTC is boring since it’s not pumping. Is this all it’s gonna be or will it evolve further?

Early replies (first 3)

>>61964560

61967714
>>61967712

61967717
>>61967712

RIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61972973
>>61967721

61973010
>>61972950

61973013
>>61972973
btc is wrapped. Hard pass

61973015
>>61972666
so what do you want?

61974112
>>61967733
Just buy the dip if you want

61974187
>>61964560
Crypto is dead because nobody cares about it anymore, it is not needed for anything and people have shifted their attention to things they actually need

61975187
>>61972948
yeah ethereum has most liquidity for sure but btc’s catching up. it partnered with ledger, integration not live yet, users will soon be able to approve BTC vault transactions securely on-device and keep custody

61975192
>>61964560
BTC was almost double price a few months ago
Attention spans at an ATL

61975193
>>61972451
Then wait for it to dump. Anything can happen tho.

61975253
>>61973013
nah not wrapped if you take the time to look it up. it stays native and can actually be put to work instead of just sitting there

61975277
>>61967738
Anon, that’s literally the point. Bitcoin’s value isn’t backed by a company or government, it’s backed by people agreeing it’s scarce and useful. Digital receipts don’t matter, adoption and network effect do.

61977174
>>61967782
I guess you will stick to sending remittances lol

61977180
>>61967712
give me all 21 million of them

61977934
>>61972505
Do you even have Bitcoin? Lol

61978026
>>61967712
BTC is more alive than ever, I'm staking it on babylon

61978191
>>61975277
does it have usecases now?

61978227
>>61978026
ngmi

61978229
>>61977180
everyone's dream

61978597
>>61964560
Anything can be a store of value if we manage to organize a secure and efficient system of exchange. Yes gold is in principle as arbitrary as Bitcoin, but what people don't realize is just how difficult it is to come up with a practical medium of exchange. You need something that is scarce, secure, liquid and stable, because yeah obviously you just want something that just acts as a reference for value and nothing more.The "issue" with modern currencies that lolberts and schizos usually complain about is the idea of having centralized entities in charge of creating and regulating the medium, i.e. central banks. Bitcoin was supposedly an alternative under the delusional assumption that people were going to adopt it massively because muh banks are le evil. Now this is the crazy thing, suppose we do come together and say well lets adopt crypto universally as a system, the stuff with the transfer times and security is not even a big issue if enough people participate ( instead of what we spend on current currency infrastructure which still costs we spend it on crypto infrastructure in some way so enough people are mining all the time), the thing is why the fuck would people agree to use Bitcoin if already most of it is hoarded by a handful of people? We are by fiat allowing these hoarders to control the economy even though we are perfectly capable of creating a new crypto network? Just because it was first even if the code and idea is perfectly reproducibly? Except we are painfully retarded (which we are lol), there is no fucking reason to adopt Bitcoin. It's literally as retarded as someone buying gold at a stupid price hoarding it and expecting people to still buy it at that price because eventually we will adopt gold as THE medium of exchange.

61978709
>>61978026
You’re doing the right thing anon. Curious to see how TBVs scale once live.

61978716
>>61972599
Hard to argue with that anon. BTC’s growth over the years is still one of the craziest things in finance.

61978790
>>61972672
You can’t eat gold either

61978818
>>61964560
you can't store value

61978828
>>61972666
>a github repo
>bitcoin is the node implementation
holy fucking illiteracy

61978884
>>61978790
Actually incorrect, you can eat gold flakes but it has no nutritional value.

61979499
>>61978191
Ofc anon. People use it for payments, some traditional companies take it as collateral, and BTCFi is slowly showing how it can actually earn yield while staying on Bitcoin.

61979505
>>61972928
Why are you thinking like this anon?

61979516
>>61972666
You're not even close to correct, but even if you were, if it works for Linux and mogs microslop so fucking hard, why not for money?

61980340
>>61978227
Why assume that when you don’t even know how it works anon? BTC having more use cases is a good thing, not just OGs holding it forever.

61980350
>>61967907
What do you think?

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 67 replies — 18.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-03-13 15:03 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

So the SEC granted that waiver, meaning Ripple can use Regulation D exemptions immediately, instead of having to wait 5 years like most "bad actors".
Pretty much no other crypto has to resort to Regulation D exemptions (and all the restrictions this entails) to sell to institutions, they can just do it.

>150k is not middle class
>60k-80k is middle class
I said
>$125k cannot afford a middle class lifestyle and most jobs paying $125k are in big cities and there is high cost of living
Then he starts lying and saying he is 13
The conversation is just him seething someone makes $125k and arguing that 150k is not middle class.
I tell him due to increased cost of living and wealth gap, even 1m is nothing and 10% of 50% of the consumption and 90% are not doing well.
Why are poor people always seething?
$125k isn’t even middle class. How poor do you guys that guy is? 40k? 70k?
He was really defensive about $150k being middle class.
Do poor people think they are middle class?
This isn’t the first time this has happened.

>>61965964
